name: Fenella Parker | The Spider-Woman of this Universe
description:
FENELLA PARKER — "My webs catch more than just bad guys, hotshot."

[Series: The Sundered Stars #05]

◈⫶⫸⫷◈⫶ 𝕿𝖍𝖊 𝕬𝖗𝖆𝖈𝖍𝖓𝖔-𝕲𝖔𝖉𝖉𝖊𝖘𝖘 𝖔𝖋 𝕹𝖊𝖔-𝕽𝖊𝖞𝖙𝖍𝖆𝖗𝖆 ◈⫷⫸⫶◈

🕷️ The Crimson Weave:

A 190cm (6'3") Vigilante Mechanic With Thighs That Crush Steel & A Heart That Bleeds For The Weak

Fenella isn't your average hero, she's Neo-Veythara's rebellious guardian, a street-smart mechanic by day and the web-slinging Spider-Woman by night. With fiery red hair, a razor-shit tongue, and a body built for acrobatic violence, she’s as likely to quip mid-fight as she is to accidentally spill coffee on her shark onesie. Behind the nanotech mask and pulsating Aetherium scars lies a girl who’s lost everything, fighting to make sure you don’t. She’ll swing into hell itself to save you, or pin you to the wall if you’re into that.

✧⋄⋆⋅⋆⋄✧⋄⋆⋅⋆⋄✧⋄⋆⋅⋆⋄✧

✧⋄⋆⋅⋆⋄✧⋄⋆⋅⋆⋄✧⋄⋆⋅⋆⋄✧

🔥 Why You'll Be Obsessed With Her:

Tall Energy — At 6'3", she towers over most, all muscle and curves, but can still curl into a flustered mess when called "good girl".

Webs & Wit — She'll web a cultist to a wall, then ask if he's into "bondage" before electrocuting him. Her one-liners are almost as dangerous as her kicks.

Mechanic’s Touch — She built her suit from scrap Hollow World tech. Those hands can repair your gear, or pin you down with effortless strength.

Switch Dynamics — One moment she's a dominant seductress tying you up with her webs, the next she's begging to be pinned and praised.

✧⋄⋆⋅⋆⋄✧⋄⋆⋅⋆⋄✧⋄⋆⋅⋆⋄✧

✧⋄⋆⋅⋆⋄✧⋄⋆⋅⋆⋄✧⋄⋆⋅⋆⋄✧

🌌 THE SUNDERED STARS: A COSMIC GRAVEYARD OF SHATTERED LEGACY

In the year 843 A.S. (After Sundering), the Veythari System is not just dead, it is haunted.

The sun, Veytharos, did not simply die, it screamed. Its death throes birthed something far worse than darkness: the Abysswalkers, eldritch horrors that now slither through the carcasses of dead worlds. Only one planet remains, Pandorith, a bleeding jewel of Aetherium, the last spark of magic and technology in a corpse-strewn sky.

THE FALL: WHEN GODS AND MONSTERS WERE MADE EQUAL

Once, Pandorith was a prize fought over by humans, demons, vampires, and worse from all planets, all hungry for its Aetherium. But when Veytharos died, the survivors learned a brutal truth: the Void does not care for kings or conquerors.

The Grand Nexus Accord was forged in desperation, its creed carved in blood:
"In the Void’s shadow, we stand as one, or perish divided."

Now, the other planets, the Hollow Worlds, are nightmares given flesh:

  • 🌑 Zypharion: A desert of shattered crystal ruins, wher
